title: balanophora fungosa subsp. fungosa
intro: Balanophora fungosa subsp. fungosa, also known as the dodder lily or Chinese dodder, is a type of parasitic flowering plant that obtains its nutrients by attaching itself to the roots of other plants. The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Balanophora fungosa subsp. fungosa include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and reduce inflammation. Balanophora fungosa subsp. fungosa is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its herbal preparations are typically consumed as a tea or added to soups and other traditional remedies.
